title: RAF Kinloss area Whitley Memorial
memorial_type: Rectangular brass wall-mounted plaque on wood backboard, erected by Station Commander
narrative: On 7 November 1940, Whitley NI440 of 19 OTU crashed into the garden of a property called Glenernie on Tolbooth Street in Forres during a training flight from RAF Kinloss. All six crew members were killed. Remarkably, despite crashing into the heart of a residential area, there were no civilian casualties. The crash occurred during the early period of 19 OTU's operations; the unit had only recently begun training bomber crews at Kinloss, and the move to the satellite airfield at Forres (RAF Balnageith) would not take place until the following year. The Whitley was the standard heavy bomber trainer in Bomber Command's OTU system during 1940-42, and 19 OTU would lose over 100 aircraft during its wartime existence. Full first names and personal details for the crew have not been traced in available sources.
memorial_details: A rectangular brass wall-mounted plaque on a wooden backboard was erected on Tolbooth Street, Forres, on 12 November 1989 by Group Captain R. H. Gould, Station Commander of RAF Kinloss. The plaque is incised and coloured black, listing all six crew members. Its current location may be at or near the Falconer Museum on Tolbooth Street. A separate memorial to 19 OTU as a whole exists nearby (see Crash 5 below).

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for the Whitley Bomber NI440 air crash memorial. Records the brass wall-mounted plaque erected by the Station Commander of RAF Kinloss in 1989 on Tolbooth Street, Forres.

external_narratives.source_site: Old Man's Thoughts and Tales (WordPress blog)
external_narratives.summary: Detailed personal research blog post about the Whitley crash in Forres and the wider history of Forres airfields. Provides context about 19 OTU operations and the crash into Glenernie's garden on Tolbooth Street.
key_details: Crash into garden of Glenernie property
No civilian casualties despite residential area

external_narratives.summary: Guide to the 19 OTU Memorial at Forres, providing broader context for the operational training unit that lost over 100 aircraft during wartime. The NI440 crash was one of the earliest losses.
key_details: 19 OTU lost over 100 aircraft during the war
Separate 19 OTU memorial exists in Forres

external_narratives.summary: Tourism and heritage page documenting RAF Kinloss history, including the role of 19 OTU in training bomber crews. Notes that Forres was closely linked to RAF operations throughout WW2.
key_details: RAF Kinloss history documented for visitors
